content_en:
Ibn ‘Umar 4 reported that the Messenger of Allah # said, “When the people of Paradise will actually be in Paradise, and the people of the Heilfire are in the Hellfire, death will be brought until it is made to stand between Paradise and the Hellfire. Then it will be slaughtered. Then a caller will call out, ‘O people of Paradise, eternal life and no death. And O people of the Hellfire, eternal life and no death.’ The people of Paradise will increase in the happiness

616 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

they were already upon, and the people of the Hellfire will increase in the grief they were already upon.” (Ahmad) Bukhaaree related the same through a different chain.

Jn another narration, Abu Hurairah # related that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “Death will be brought as a male sheep that is Anilah (its whiteness is more than its blackness), and then it will be made to stand between Paradise and the Hellfire. Then He will say, ‘O people of Paradise,’ and they will stretch out their necks, looking. And He will say, ‘O people of the Hellfire,’ and they will stretch out their necks, looking, and they will think that relief has come to them. Then it (death) will be slaughtered, and it will be said, ‘Eternal life and no death.” (Ahiad) In its form, this chain is Ghareeb,

Anas + related that the Prophet #s said, “Death will be brought on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be made to stand between Paradise and the Hellfire. Then it will be slaughtered. Then it will be said, ‘O people of Paradise, eternal life and no death. And O people of the Hellfire, eternal life and no death.”” (Related by Al-Haafiz Abu Bakr Al-Bazzaar) Then Al-Bazzaar said, “We do not know it to be related from Anas except through this chain.”

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 617

618 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 619

A Description Of The People Of Paradise And Of The Bliss That Is Found In Paradise;

Allah % said, wie | SKS EN Q we Wel <ash ae Vefesb Ab peak a eS oh Je eis GN GI 3, Ge ol SB 5 tis Sail Uis3 0 Sus

Sich! 71 ee vee Aw bs EN me Ls a

“And those who kept their duties to their Lord (Al-Muttaqoon) will be led to Paradise in groups, till, when they reach it, and its gates will be opened (before their arrival for their reception) and its keepers will say: “Salamun Alikum (peace be upon you)! You have done well, so enter here to abide therein.” And they will say: “All the praises and thanks be to Allah #% who has fulfilled His promise to us and has made us inherit (this) land. We can dwell in Paradise where we will; how excellent a reward for the (pious good) workers!"T1

And Allah # said: GON A SE gh kp

‘And (Eden) Paradise (everlasting Gardens), whose doors will be opened for them’!

And Allah # said:

gohe Wee eae aan

<a, sae 2 rae rae it he ot , = ON oe La iow Fi gS “And angels shall enter unto them from every gate (saying):

“Salamun ‘Alikum (peace be upon you) for that you persevered in

620 Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations

patience! ” Excellent indeed is the final home!’

Some of the Ahaadeeth we have previously related mention that, when the believers reach the gate of Paradise, they will find it to be closed, Then they (look for someone to) intercede to Allah ‘Azza WaJall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty), so that it is opened for them.

In the hadith of the horn it is mentioned that they will go to Adam 8e2, then to Noah #4, then to Ibraaheem 3&8, then to Moosa See, and then to ‘Eesa #8) — with each one of them turning away from accepting (the task of interceding) (this has preceded in authentic narrations). Then they will go to the Messenger of Allah 2. He will go and rap the ring of the gate of Paradise, The Keeper (of Paradise) will say, “Who?” He 2 will say, “Muhammad.” He will say, “For you I have been ordered not to open to anyone before you.’ Then he #3 will enter and intercede to Allah for the believers to enter the Abode of Honor. Allah # will grant his intercession. Then he ¢ will be the first to enter Paradise from among the Prophets, and his nation will be the first to enter it from among the nations.

The following is established in As-sahih: “I am the first to intercede (and have his intercession granted) for Paradise and the first to rap {at its Gate).” We will also relate the hadith: “The key to Paradise is Laailaaha illallah (None has the right to be worshipped but Allah),” The Leader of the Believers, ‘Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab #s, related that the Messenger of Allah # said, “Whoever performs Wudoo {ablution), and performs it well, and then raises his gaze to the sky and says, ‘I bear witness that none has the right to be worshipped but Allah alone, and He has no partner, and I bear witness that Muhammad is His slave and Messenger,’ the eight gates of Paradise will be opened for him, and he may enter whichever one of them he wishes.” (Related by Ahmad, Muslim, and the Compilers of the “Sunan.”)

Sahl ibn Sa’ad 2 reported that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Verily, Paradise has a gate that is called Ar-Rayyaan. The fasting ones will be called to it on the Day of Resurrection. It will be said, ‘Where are the fasting ones?’ And after they enter it, it will be closed, and no one other than them will enter through it.” (Ahmad)

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 621

Sahl ibn Sa’d & reported that the Messenger of Allah 28 said, “In Paradise there are eight gates; one of those gates is called ArRayyaan; none will enter it except for the fasting ones.” (Related by At-Tabaraanee) Al-Bukhaarce related it through Sa’eed ibn Abee Maryam from Sahl #, and Muslim related it through another chain, bat also from Sahl #.

Abu Hurairah 2 related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “Whoever spends two of the same kind from his wealth in the way of Allah, will be called from the gates of Paradise, and Paradise has eight gates. Whoever was from the people of prayer will be called from the gate of prayer. Whoever was from the people of charity, will be called from the gate of charity. And whoever was from the people of fasting will be called from the gate of Ar-Rayyaan.” Abu Bakr said, “...Will anyone be called from all of them, O Messenger of Allah?” He #¢ said, “Yes, and I hope that you will be from them.” (Ahmad) Bukhaaree and Muslim related it from the hadith of AzZuthree from Abu Hurairah ss, and in the hadith of Sufyaan from Abu Hurairah 2s.

The following is mentioned in a hadith related by ‘Utbah ibn “Abdullah As-Salamee .& from the Prophet :#% in it the fighting of the sincere one, the fighting of the sinner, and fighting of the hypocrite (their fighting in battles against the enemies) are discussed. And it in he #% said, “Paradise has eight gates, and verily, the sword is a strong eraser of sins, but it does not erase hypocrisy.” (Related by Al-Baihagee)

622 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

distance between Makkah and Basrah.” (Bukhaaree and Muslin)

Khaalid ibn ‘Umair Al-’Adwee related that ‘Utbah ibn Ghazwaan addressed the people, and after he praised and 2xalted Allah 3é, he said, “As for what follows: verily, the world is announcing its being cut off, and it has turned away, running. All that is left from it are remains like the remains of a container (after its contents have been consumed) - its owner will pour it out. Verily, you are moving from it to the abode that never passes away; then go with the best of your deeds. Ithas been mentioned to us that the distance between two gates from the gates of Paradise is the traveling distance of 40 years. Anda day will come upon it that it (that area at the gates of Paradise) will be overfilled and crowded.” (Muslim)

Mu’aawiyah related from his father that the Messenger of Allah 23 said, “You are the completion of 70 nations — the last of them and the most honorable of them with Allah, And the distance between two gates of the gates of Paradise is the traveling distance of 40 years; and a day will come upon it (ie. that area) that it will be overfilled.” (Ahmad) Al-Baihagee related it through a different chain with the wording, “The traveling distance of 7 years.”
content_id:
Ibnu 'Umar رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Ketika penghuni Surga sudah benar-benar di Surga, dan penghuni Neraka ada di Neraka, kematian akan dibawa hingga dibuat berdiri di antara Surga dan Neraka. Kemudian kematian itu akan disembelih. Kemudian seorang penyeru akan berseru, 'Wahai penghuni Surga, kehidupan abadi dan tidak ada kematian. Dan wahai penghuni Neraka, kehidupan abadi dan tidak ada kematian.' Penghuni Surga akan bertambah dalam kebahagiaan

616 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

yang sudah ada pada mereka, dan penghuni Neraka akan bertambah dalam kesedihan yang sudah ada pada mereka." (Ahmad) Bukhari meriwayatkan hal yang sama melalui rantai yang berbeda.

Dalam riwayat lain, Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Kematian akan dibawa sebagai seekor domba jantan yang Amilah (keputihannya lebih banyak dari kegelapannya), kemudian akan dibuat berdiri di antara Surga dan Neraka. Kemudian Dia akan berfirman, 'Wahai penghuni Surga,' dan mereka akan mengulurkan leher-leher mereka, memandang. Dan Dia akan berfirman, 'Wahai penghuni Neraka,' dan mereka akan mengulurkan leher-leher mereka, memandang, dan mereka akan mengira bahwa keringanan telah datang kepada mereka. Kemudian kematian itu (kematian) akan disembelih, dan akan dikatakan, 'Kehidupan abadi dan tidak ada kematian.'" (Ahmad) Dalam bentuknya, rantai ini Gharib.

Anas رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Kematian akan dibawa pada Hari Kiamat, dan akan dibuat berdiri di antara Surga dan Neraka. Kemudian kematian itu akan disembelih. Kemudian akan dikatakan, 'Wahai penghuni Surga, kehidupan abadi dan tidak ada kematian. Dan wahai penghuni Neraka, kehidupan abadi dan tidak ada kematian.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafiz Abu Bakr Al-Bazzaar) Kemudian Al-Bazzaar berkata, "Kami tidak mengetahuinya diriwayatkan dari Anas kecuali melalui rantai ini."

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 617

618 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 619

Gambaran tentang Penghuni Surga dan Kenikmatan yang Terdapat di Surga;

Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Dan orang-orang yang bertakwa kepada Tuhan mereka (Al-Muttaqoon) akan dibawa ke Surga secara berkelompok, hingga, ketika mereka tiba di sana, dan pintu-pintunya akan dibuka (sebelum kedatangan mereka untuk menyambut mereka) dan para penjaganya akan berkata: 'Assalaamu'alaikum (keselamatan atas kalian)! Kalian telah berbuat baik, maka masuklah ke sini untuk tinggal di dalamnya selamanya.' Dan mereka akan berkata: 'Segala puji dan syukur bagi Allah عز وجل yang telah memenuhi janji-Nya kepada kami dan telah membuat kami mewarisi (ini) negeri. Kami dapat tinggal di Surga di mana kami menghendaki; sungguh suatu balasan yang sangat baik bagi para pekerja (yang saleh)!'"

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Dan (Eden) Surga (taman-taman abadi), yang pintu-pintunya akan dibuka untuk mereka."

Dan Allah عز وجل berfirman:

"Dan para malaikat akan masuk kepada mereka dari setiap pintu (seraya berkata): 'Assalaamu'alaikum (keselamatan atas kalian) karena kalian bersabar!

620 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Sungguh sangat indah tempat tinggal yang terakhir itu!'"

Sebagian dari hadis-hadis yang telah kami riwayatkan sebelumnya menyebutkan bahwa, ketika orang-orang beriman tiba di gerbang Surga, mereka akan mendapatinya tertutup. Kemudian mereka (mencari seseorang untuk) memohon syafaat kepada Allah 'Azza Wa-Jall (Bagi-Nya Kemuliaan dan Keagungan), agar pintu itu dibuka untuk mereka.

Dalam hadis tentang sangkakala disebutkan bahwa mereka akan pergi kepada Adam عليه السلام, kemudian kepada Nuh عليه السلام, kemudian kepada Ibrahim عليه السلام, kemudian kepada Musa عليه السلام, dan kemudian kepada 'Eesa عليه السلام — dengan masing-masing dari mereka berpaling dari menerima (tugas memberi syafaat) (ini telah disebutkan dalam narasi-narasi yang sahih). Kemudian mereka akan pergi kepada Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم. Beliau akan pergi dan mengetuk cincin pintu Surga. Penjaga (Surga) akan berkata, "Siapa?" Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم akan berkata, "Muhammad." Dia akan berkata, "Untukmu aku diperintahkan untuk tidak membuka kepada siapa pun sebelummu." Kemudian beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم akan masuk dan memberi syafaat kepada Allah agar orang-orang beriman masuk ke Tempat Kemuliaan. Allah عز وجل akan mengabulkan syafaatnya. Kemudian beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم akan menjadi yang pertama masuk Surga di antara para Nabi, dan umatnya akan menjadi yang pertama memasukinya di antara umat-umat.

Berikut ini ditetapkan dalam As-Shahih: "Aku adalah yang pertama memberi syafaat (dan mendapat syafaatnya dikabulkan) untuk Surga dan yang pertama mengetuk (Pintunya)." Kami juga akan meriwayatkan hadis: "Kunci Surga adalah Laa ilaaha illallah (Tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi selain Allah)." Pemimpin Kaum Mukminin, 'Umar ibn Al-Khattaab رضي الله عنه, meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Barang siapa berwudu, dan melakukannya dengan baik, kemudian mengangkat pandangannya ke langit dan berkata, 'Aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak diibadahi selain Allah semata, dan Dia tidak memiliki sekutu, dan aku bersaksi bahwa Muhammad adalah hamba dan utusan-Nya,' kedelapan pintu Surga akan dibuka untuknya, dan dia boleh memasuki mana saja yang dia kehendaki." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad, Muslim, dan para penyusun 'Sunan.')

Sahl ibn Sa'ad رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Sesungguhnya, Surga memiliki sebuah pintu yang disebut Ar-Rayyan. Orang-orang yang berpuasa akan dipanggil ke sana pada Hari Kiamat. Akan dikatakan, 'Di mana orang-orang yang berpuasa?' Dan setelah mereka memasukinya, pintu itu akan ditutup, dan tidak ada seorang pun selain mereka yang akan masuk melaluinya." (Ahmad)

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 621

Sahl ibn Sa'd رضي الله عنه melaporkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Di Surga ada delapan pintu; satu dari pintu-pintu itu disebut Ar-Rayyan; tidak ada yang akan memasukinya kecuali orang-orang yang berpuasa." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabaraanee) Al-Bukhari meriwayatkannya melalui Sa'eed ibn Abee Maryam dari Sahl رضي الله عنه, dan Muslim meriwayatkannya melalui rantai lain, juga dari Sahl رضي الله عنه.

Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Barang siapa menginfakkan dua dari jenis yang sama dari hartanya di jalan Allah, akan dipanggil dari pintu-pintu Surga, dan Surga memiliki delapan pintu. Barang siapa berasal dari orang-orang shalat akan dipanggil dari pintu shalat. Barang siapa berasal dari orang-orang yang bersedekah, akan dipanggil dari pintu sedekah. Dan barang siapa berasal dari orang-orang yang berpuasa akan dipanggil dari pintu Ar-Rayyan." Abu Bakr berkata, "...Apakah ada yang akan dipanggil dari semua pintu, wahai Rasulullah?" Beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Ya, dan aku berharap bahwa engkau akan termasuk dari mereka." (Ahmad) Bukhari dan Muslim meriwayatkannya dari hadis Az-Zuhree dari Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه, dan dalam hadis Sufyaan dari Abu Hurairah رضي الله عنه.

Berikut ini disebutkan dalam sebuah hadis yang diriwayatkan oleh 'Utbah ibn 'Abdullah As-Salmee رضي الله عنه dari Nabi صلى الله عليه وسلم; di dalamnya peperangan orang yang ikhlas, peperangan orang yang berdosa, dan peperangan orang munafik (peperangan mereka dalam peperangan melawan musuh) dibahas. Dan di dalamnya beliau صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Surga memiliki delapan pintu, dan sesungguhnya, pedang adalah penghapus dosa yang kuat, tetapi ia tidak menghapus kemunafikan." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Baihaqee)

622 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

jarak antara Mekkah dan Basrah." (Bukhari dan Muslim)

Khaalid ibn 'Umair Al-'Adwee meriwayatkan bahwa 'Utbah ibn Ghazwaan berkhutbah kepada orang-orang, dan setelah memuji dan mengagungkan Allah عز وجل, dia berkata, "Adapun apa yang mengikutinya: sesungguhnya, dunia mengumumkan terputusnya dirinya, dan ia telah berbalik, berlari. Yang tersisa darinya hanyalah sisa-sisa seperti sisa-sisa wadah (setelah isinya habis dikonsumsi) - pemiliknya akan menuangkannya. Sesungguhnya, kalian bergerak darinya menuju tempat tinggal yang tidak pernah berlalu; maka pergilah dengan amal terbaik kalian. Telah disebutkan kepada kami bahwa jarak antara dua pintu dari pintu-pintu Surga adalah jarak perjalanan 40 tahun. Dan suatu hari akan tiba padanya bahwa (area di pintu-pintu Surga itu) akan dipenuhi dan sesak." (Muslim)

Mu'aawiyah meriwayatkan dari ayahnya bahwa Rasulullah صلى الله عليه وسلم bersabda, "Kalian adalah penyempurnaan 70 umat — yang terakhir dari mereka dan yang paling mulia di antara mereka di sisi Allah. Dan jarak antara dua pintu dari pintu-pintu Surga adalah jarak perjalanan 40 tahun; dan suatu hari akan tiba padanya (yaitu area itu) bahwa ia akan dipenuhi." (Ahmad) Al-Baihaqee meriwayatkannya melalui rantai yang berbeda dengan lafaz, "Jarak perjalanan 7 tahun."
content_ar: Ibn ‘عمر 4 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “عندما the people of الجنة will actually be in الجنة, and the people of the Heilfire are in the النار, الموت will be brought حتى it is made to stand between الجنة and the النار. Then it will be slaughtered. Then a caller will call out, ‘O people of الجنة, eternal الحياة and no الموت. And O people of the النار, eternal الحياة and no الموت.’ The people of الجنة will increase in the happiness 616 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations they were already upon, and the people of the النار will increase in the الحزن they were already upon.” (أحمد) Bukhaaree related the same through a different chain. Jn another narration, أبو هريرة # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “الموت will be brought as a male sheep that is Anilah (its whiteness is more than its blackness), and then it will be made to stand between الجنة and the النار. Then He will say, ‘O people of الجنة,’ and they will stretch out their necks, looking. And He will say, ‘O people of the النار,’ and they will stretch out their necks, looking, and they will think that الفرج has come to them. Then it (الموت) will be slaughtered, and it will be said, ‘Eternal الحياة and no الموت.” (Ahiad) In its form, this chain is Ghareeb, أنس + related that النبي ﷺ #s said, “الموت will be brought on the Day of البعث, and it will be made to stand between الجنة and the النار. Then it will be slaughtered. Then it will be said, ‘O people of الجنة, eternal الحياة and no الموت. And O people of the النار, eternal الحياة and no الموت.”” (Related by Al-Haafiz أبو بكر Al-Bazzaar) Then Al-Bazzaar said, “We do not know it to be related from أنس except through this chain.” الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 617 618 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 619 A Description Of The People Of الجنة And Of The Bliss That Is Found In الجنة; الله % said, wie | SKS EN Q we Wel <ash ae Vefesb Ab peak a eS oh Je eis GN GI 3, Ge ol SB 5 tis Sail Uis3 0 Sus Sich! 71 ee vee Aw bs EN me Ls a “And those who kept their duties to their Lord (Al-Muttaqoon) will be led to الجنة in groups, till, عندما they reach it, and its gates will be opened (قبل their arrival for their reception) and its keepers will say: “Salamun Alikum (peace be upon you)! You have done well, لذا enter here to abide therein.” And they will say: “All the praises and thanks be to الله #% who has fulfilled His promise to us and has made us inherit (this) land. We can dwell in الجنة where we will; how excellent a الأجر for the (pious الخير) workers!"T1 And الله # said: GON A SE gh kp ‘And (Eden) الجنة (everlasting Gardens), whose doors will be opened for them’! And الله # said: gohe Wee eae aan <a, sae 2 rae rae it he ot , = ON oe La iow Fi gS “And الملائكة shall enter unto them from every gate (saying): “Salamun ‘Alikum (peace be upon you) for that you persevered in 620 الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations الصبر! ” Excellent indeed is the final home!’ Some of the Ahaadeeth we have previously related mention that, عندما the المؤمنون reach the gate of الجنة, they will find it to be closed, Then they (look for someone to) intercede to الله ‘Azza WaJall (To Him belongs Might and Majesty), لذا that it is opened for them. In the الحديث of the horn ورد that they will go to آدم 8e2, then to نوح #4, then to Ibraaheem 3&8, then to Moosa See, and then to ‘Eesa #8) — with each one of them turning away from accepting (the task of interceding) (this has preceded in authentic narrations). Then they will go to الرسول ﷺ of الله 2. He will go and rap the ring of the gate of الجنة, The Keeper (of الجنة) will say, “Who?” He 2 will say, “محمد ﷺ.” He will say, “For you I have been ordered not to open to anyone قبل you.’ Then he #3 will enter and intercede to الله for the المؤمنون to enter the Abode of الشرف. الله # will grant his intercession. Then he ¢ will be the أولاً to enter الجنة from among the الأنبياء, and his nation will be the أولاً to enter it from among the nations. The following is established in As-صحيح: “I am the أولاً to intercede (and have his intercession granted) for الجنة and the أولاً to rap {at its Gate).” We will أيضاً relate the الحديث: “The الرئيسي to الجنة is Laailaaha illallah (None has the الحق to be worshipped but الله),” The Leader of the المؤمنون, ‘عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab #s, related that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, “Whoever performs الوضوء {ablution), and performs it well, and then raises his gaze to the sky and says, ‘I bear witness that none has the الحق to be worshipped but الله alone, and He has no partner, and I bear witness that محمد ﷺ is His slave and Messenger,’ the eight gates of الجنة will be opened for him, and he may enter whichever one of them he wishes.” (Related by أحمد, مسلم, and the Compilers of the “Sunan.”) Sahl ibn Sa’ad 2 reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “Verily, الجنة has a gate that is called Ar-Rayyaan. The الصيام ones will be called to it on the Day of البعث. It will be said, ‘Where are the الصيام ones?’ And بعد they enter it, it will be closed, and no one other than them will enter through it.” (أحمد) الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 621 Sahl ibn Sa’d & reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله 28 said, “In الجنة there are eight gates; one of those gates is called ArRayyaan; none will enter it except for the الصيام ones.” (Related by At-Tabaraanee) Al-Bukhaarce related it through Sa’eed ibn Abee Maryam from Sahl #, and مسلم related it through another chain, bat أيضاً from Sahl #. أبو هريرة 2 related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “Whoever spends two of the same kind from his الثروة in the الطريق of الله, will be called from the gates of الجنة, and الجنة has eight gates. Whoever was from the people of الصلاة will be called from the gate of الصلاة. Whoever was from the people of الصدقة, will be called from the gate of الصدقة. And whoever was from the people of الصيام will be called from the gate of Ar-Rayyaan.” أبو بكر said, “...Will anyone be called from all of them, O Messenger of الله?” He #¢ said, “Yes, and I الأمل that you will be from them.” (أحمد) Bukhaaree and مسلم related it from the الحديث of AzZuthree from أبو هريرة ss, and in the الحديث of Sufyaan from أبو هريرة 2s. The following is mentioned in a الحديث related by ‘Utbah ibn “Abdullah As-Salamee .& from النبي ﷺ :#% in it the fighting of the sincere one, the fighting of the sinner, and fighting of the hypocrite (their fighting in battles against the enemies) are discussed. And it in he #% said, “الجنة has eight gates, and verily, the sword is a strong eraser of الذنوب, but it does not erase hypocrisy.” (Related by Al-Baihagee) 622 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations distance between مكة المكرمة and Basrah.” (Bukhaaree and Muslin) Khaalid ibn ‘Umair Al-’Adwee related that ‘Utbah ibn Ghazwaan addressed the people, and بعد he praised and 2xalted الله 3é, قال, “أما what follows: verily, the الدنيا is announcing its being cut off, and it has turned away, running. All that is left from it are remains like the remains of a container (بعد its contents have been consumed) - its owner will pour it out. Verily, you are moving from it to the abode that never passes away; then go with the best of your deeds. Ithas been mentioned to us that the distance between two gates from the gates of الجنة is the traveling distance of 40 years. Anda day will come upon it that it (that area at the gates of الجنة) will be overfilled and crowded.” (مسلم) Mu’aawiyah related from his الأب that الرسول ﷺ of الله 23 said, “You are the completion of 70 nations — the last of them and the most honorable of them with الله, And the distance between two gates of the gates of الجنة is the traveling distance of 40 years; and a day will come upon it (ie. that area) that it will be overfilled.” (أحمد) Al-Baihagee related it through a different chain with the wording, “The traveling distance of 7 years.”
